summary: Made the Path Map Proof Cache progressively expandable so operators can reveal more artifact-preview cards in-place without overwhelming the first scan.

queries: progressive disclosure show more cards dashboard UX pattern
load more cards dashboard progressive disclosure UX
expandable activity feed show more items UX
pattern_notes: Load More gives operators explicit control over progressive exploration instead of forcing endless content.
Progressive disclosure keeps the first scan compact while allowing deeper review on request.
Expandable sections should preserve context and remove or update the control when all content is visible.

ui_contract: Default the Proof Cache to three cards per lane.
Reveal three additional proof cards per click using browser-local state only.
Show visible proof count against total proof-preview count.
Keep Proof Cache cards clickable into the existing Event Proof lens.
Keep the reveal action responsive inside the Path Map bottom grid.

red_route.evidence: Before implementation, Proof Cache showed 3 cards, no data-expand-proof-cache control, and no Reveal more proof text.

green_route.evidence: Proof Cache started at 3 of 38 previews visible, revealed to 6 of 38 after one click, and an expanded proof card updated Event Proof to Digital products local post-polish readiness with 4 preview lines.

responsive.evidence: All tested viewports revealed to 6 proof cards, updated Event Proof after selecting an expanded card, kept document width equal to viewport width, and reported no bad rectangles, console errors, or failed requests.
